frameworks.name: simultaneous vs sequential presentation
frameworks.one_line: Comparing concurrent versus serial stimulus exposure for memorization in VR.

synopsis.core_question: Does concurrent versus sequential VR presentation of constellation drawings and labels affect memorization?
key_assumptions: memorization can be probed by partial-cue recall (missing labels or drawings)
within-subjects design controls individual memory differences
manipulations: presentation mode: all 14 constellations at once vs one by one
test cue type: half labels removed vs half drawings removed
key_findings: simultaneous presentation produced better retention than sequential
both presentation modes reached usable accuracy
interpretations: stimulus concurrence benefits constellation memorization in VR
synopsis.out_of_scope_note: VR educational memorization study; HCI/learning-design focus, not core computational neuroscience but adjacent to memory/encoding paradigms.
